mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market demand and future consumption capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2011 payroll level 133,999k indicates ongoing post-recession recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 150k persons in month t but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 4.0% while inflation (CPI) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, signaling real rate compression that reduces equity discount rates and supports forward equity valuations over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.5% as of Feb 2011, CPIAUCSL at 196.1 indicating inflation pressure
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC between the interaction signal and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism produces negative returns in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 15% month-over-month while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or negative, signaling demand contraction that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 15% month-over-month AND RSAFS growth < 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,042k units in Jan 2011, RSAFS at 350,106M in Feb 2011
failure_condition: The joint signal (both conditions met) produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining above the prior-year level, indicating supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND INDPRO year-over-year change > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 119.678 in Feb 2011, reflecting post-recession recovery plateau
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO declines month-over-month despite year-over-year growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2% in a single month, signaling deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers equity risk-off positioning and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% in Feb 2011, near post-recession lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession risk
failure_condition: UNRATE exceeds 5.2% but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter annualized rate falls below 2.0%, signaling demand weakness that reduces forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,088.6B (2017 dollars) as of Q4 2010, reflecting post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Annualized GDP growth falls below 2.0% but S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of such quarters, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or below 3.5% while CPI index level rises above 195, creating a negative real rate environment that compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through portfolio reallocation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.5% and CPIAUCSL at 196.1 as of Feb 2011
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: Both conditions met but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 2,000k units, signaling sustained consumer and residential demand that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved near-term earnings visibility.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1.0% AND HOUST > 2000k units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 350,106M and HOUST at 2,042k in early 2011
failure_condition: Both conditions met but S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.
